# Memory Maintenance

- Shared memories are generated, reviewed, committed, and read-only.
- Local memories are owned exclusively by the deterministic engine and are never committed.
- The model authors only `local/current-task`; the engine validates candidates and renders managed local views.
- Unknown visible local memories are quarantined without content interpretation and listed in `local/unresolved`.
- Raw episodes, quarantine archives, and machine state are hidden and must never contain transcripts, payloads, logs, credentials, or customer data.
- Shared memories summarize canonical repository authorities; they never override AGENTS or architecture documents.
- Navigate through `mem:core` and use `mem:` references so integrity checks can detect stale links.
- Regenerate with `pnpm serena:memories`; do not hand-edit generated files.
